channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Brazilian police arrest 5 officers in alleged 2022 coup plot to kill President Lula, others
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil ’s federal police arrested Tuesday five officers accused of plotting a coup that included plans to overthrow the government following the 2022 elections and kill then-President-elect Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: On board with Senegal’s navy as it searches for migrants on a popular but deadly route toward Europe
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — As dawn arrived off the coast of Senegal, navy commander Assane Seye scanned the horizon.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE RISEMBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Croatia to hold a presidential election on Dec. 29
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia will hold a presidential election on Dec. 29, Prime Minister Andrej Plenković announced Tuesday. The government is expected on Thursday to formally schedule the vote. If no candidate wins an outright majority on Dec. 29, a runoff vote will take place two weeks later.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran defies international pressure, increasing its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium, UN says
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Iran has defied international demands to rein in its nuclear program and has increased its stockpile of uranium enriched to near weapons-grade levels, according to a confidential report by the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog seen Tuesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Son of Norway's crown princess arrested on suspicion of rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was arrested on suspicion of rape in Oslo late last night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Croatia to hold a presidential election on Dec. 29
item.description: Croatia's prime minister says the European Union country will hold a presidential election on Dec. 29.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine fires several US-made longer-range missiles into Russia for the first time
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine fired several American-supplied longer-range missiles into Russia, officials said Tuesday, marking the first time Kyiv used the weapons that way in 1,000 days of war.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 1,000 days of war in Ukraine as Zelenskyy doubles down on aerial options with ATACMS, drones and missiles
item.description: Ukraine marks 1,000 days of war with Russia on Tuesday as President Zelenskyy eyes plan to develop thousands of long-range drones and military targets inside Russia using US-supplied ATACMS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World's hottest calendar drops 2025 edition, proceeds going to Hurricane Helene, Milton efforts
item.description: This year, the funds collected from calendar sales will support those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, to provide immediate relief and aid to impacted communities.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukraine fires US-made ATACMS at Russia for first time
item.description: Ukraine fired U.S.-made long-range missiles inside of Russia on Tuesday for the first time since a restriction was lifted by Washington, according to Russian officials.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Brazilian police arrest 5 officers in alleged 2022 coup plot to kill President Lula, others
item.description: Brazil's federal police have arrested five officers accused of plotting a coup that included plans to overthrow the government following the 2022 elections and kill then-President-elect Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Sá Pessoa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Putin lowers threshold for Russian nuclear weapons use after Biden's Ukraine missile decision
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Tuesday lowered the threshold for nuclear weapons use, just two days after the U.S. authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside of Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia resigns, ending unrest
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — The separatist leader of Georgia's breakaway region of Abkhazia resigned on Tuesday after days of unrest, in which the opposition and its supporters seized key government buildings and at least 14 people were injured in clashes with police.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police in Georgia's capital break up a tent camp set up by protesters demanding a new election
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Police in Georgia's capital early on Tuesday moved in to break up a tent camp that demonstrators set up on a central thoroughfare to protest the results of last month's parliamentary election and demand a new vote. The Oct.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin signs revised doctrine lowering threshold for nuclear response if Russia is attacked
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a revised nuclear doctrine stating that any attack on Russia supported by a country with nuclear power could potentially trigger a nuclear response.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US envoy says Israel-Hezbollah truce is 'within our grasp' as Gaza food crisis worsens after looting
item.description: US envoy Amos Hochstein says an agreement to end the Israel-Hezbollah war is “within our grasp” after meeting with officials in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb, Wafaa Shurafa And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 45 pro-democracy activists get 4 to 10 years in prison in Hong Kong's biggest national security case
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Forty-five ex-lawmakers and activists were sentenced to four to 10 years in prison Tuesday in Hong Kong’s biggest national security case under a Beijing-imposed law that crushed a once-thriving pro-democracy movement.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG and ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands crowd New Zealand's Parliament grounds in support of Māori rights
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — As tens of thousands crowded the streets in New Zealand’s capital, Wellington, on Tuesday, the throng of people, flags aloft, had the air of a festival or a parade rather than a protest.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: US envoy holds talks in Lebanon on Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire proposal
item.description: A U.S. envoy has held talks in Beirut with Lebanese officials about a possible cease-fire in the Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lula urges G20 members to take action to slow global warming
item.description: Brazil’s president has opened the second day of a meeting of the world’s 20 major economies by calling for more action to slow global warming, saying developed nations must speed up their initiatives to reduce harmful emissions.
item.dc:creator: Mauricio Savarese, Eléonore Hughes And David Biller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Foul play ruled out month after body of Walmart employee found inside walk-in oven at Canada store
item.description: Authorities in Canada have determined there was no foul play leading to the death of a Walmart employee whose body was found inside a walk-in oven of the store last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abuse survivors urge the Vatican to globalize the zero-tolerance policy it approved in the US
item.description: ROME (AP) — Survivors of clergy sexual abuse urged the Vatican on Monday to expand its zero-tolerance policy that it approved for the U.S. Catholic Church in 2002 to the rest of the world, arguing that children everywhere should be protected from predator priests. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cruz says UN draft resolution against Israel is aimed at undermining incoming Trump admin, GOP Congress
item.description: In the closing weeks of the Biden administration, there are new concerns in Israel and among supporters of the Jewish state that the Biden administration will seek to punish Israel at the United Nations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Today he is a high school football player. Soon he'll be a Buddhist lama in the Himalayas
item.description: ISANTI, Minn. (AP) — The young Buddhist lama sat on a throne near an altar decorated with flowers, fruits and golden statues of the Buddha, watching the celebrations of his 18th birthday in silence, with a faint smile.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu confirms Israel strike against Iran hit nuke program during October retaliatory strikes
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed that part of Iran's nuclear program was degraded after IDF strikes on military facilities in and around Tehran in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK hits Iran with new sanctions for supplying missiles to Russia
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K. government hit Iran with new sanctions Monday for sending ballistic missiles and other weapons to Russia to support the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden announces new sanctions against 'extremist' Israeli settlers
item.description: The Biden administration on Monday announced sanctions against what it described as Israeli citizens and entities involved in the extremist settler movement in the West Bank, part of U.S. efforts to curb escalating violence targeting Palestinians in the territory.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Bevy of immigration programs at risk under Trump mass deportation plan
item.description: Immigrants with humanitarian or temporary legal status in the United States are at risk of being rendered effectively undocumented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: VIDEO: Severe flight turbulence on Florida-bound flight sends person airborne, requires plane inspection
item.description: A Miami-bound flight turned frightening after the plane experienced severe turbulence, causing a diversion and requiring inspection of the plane.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy: Russia using Iranian weapons in 'massive' attack targeting energy infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian forces say Russia launched over 200 drones and missiles against the nation's power and energy infrastructure this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists in Argentina unearth oldest tadpole fossil ever discovered
item.description: A search for dinosaur fossils unintentionally shifted into a discovery of the oldest tadpole ever discovered when a team exploring in Argentina came across the find.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alex Soros, Jared Kushner reportedly obsessed with Albania
item.description: Alex Soros, whose father is Hungarian, reportedly travels to the capital Tirana so often he has called it “my second home.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: New search for MH370 Malaysia Airlines flight could begin soon
item.description: Flight MH370 vanished on March 8, 2014, shortly after departing Kuala Lumpur for Beijing with 239 people aboard.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation
